creator: cvs-src@yandex.ru
text:
William Grzybowski wrote on 06.03.2013 01:04:
> On Tue, Mar 5, 2013 at 6:00 PM, Ruslan Makhmatkhanov <cvs-src@yandex.ru> wrote:
>>
>> Checked them all. Only three needs patching:
>> ./www/py-rhodecode
>> ./devel/py-celery
>> ./devel/py-TGScheduler
>>
>> All the other do not have strict dependency on 1.5 (or just define !2.0) or do not need dateutil at all like devel/py-mwlib (patch sent to maintainer). Please fix two of your ports and I will deal with py-TGScheduler. That's will be all that's needed :)
>
>
> I have only one, rhodecode, celery is not mine (although I could
> maintain that one as well). However I was waiting for ports/175372
> before doing so.

Hmm, indeed, sorry. I'll contact celery maintainer then. But why to not 
update the patch from ports/175372 to include datetime change? It will 
break at tinderbox check in it's current state... And it's now time to 
ping culot@ about the PR, because he seems wait for dependent 
ports/172898 that was committed couple of days ago.

-- 
Regards,
Ruslan

Tinderboxing kills... the drives.
